original: Baldwin V becomes Count of Flanders
Burchard becomes bishop of Utrecht
English peasant uprising begins in Essex
Jerome of Prague burned at the stake for heresy by church Council of Constance
The Battle of Lipany (also called the Battle of Česky Brod), ending Taborites influence
French troops driven out of Genoa
University of Marburg (Germany) founded
(-31st) The first battle of Öland (between the islands of Gotland and Öland): Lübeck & Denmark beat Sweden
Sea battle at Lillo Belgium (Adolf Van Haemstede vs Louis de Boisot)
Earl Adolf of Nieuwenaar and Meurs becomes viceroy of Gelderland
Spanish troops in Zutphen surrender to Dutch and English forces under Maurice of Nassau
An explosion at the Wanggongchang Gunpowder Factory in Beijing destroys part of the city and kills 20,000 people
The Treaty of Fontainebleau signed between Maximilian I, Elector of Bavaria, and the Kingdom of France, establishing a secret alliance between them during the Thirty Years' War.
Spain & Netherlands sign temporary cease fire
Benjamin Tower of Philadelphia publishes 1st daily newspaper in US
French revolutionary Georges Couthon chosen to be a member of French Committee the Salut Public
Napoleonic Wars: War of the Sixth Coalition - the Treaty of Paris (1814) is signed returning French borders to their 1792 extent.
James Boyd patents Rubber Fire Hose

George Wilson and Joe LaRoche betray a planned slave revolt organized by Denmark Vesey in Charleston, South Carolina, confirming an earlier warning from Peter Prioleau. 35 Black people are later hanged. (date is approximate)
Evariste Galois give his theory on free assembly (dies in duel May 31)
The Rideau Canal in eastern Ontario is opened. Oldest continuously operated canal system in North America.
Second Battle at Gioto: Sardinia-Piemonte beats Austrians
Treaty of Guadalupe Hidalgo between the United States and Mexico comes into force, giving California, Nevada, Utah, and most of Colorado, New Mexico, and Arizona to the US in return for $15 million
William G. Young of Baltimore, Maryland, receives a patent for “An Improvement in Ice-Cream Freezers”
Kansas-Nebraska Act repeals Missouri Compromise creating the territories of Kansas and Nebraska
Hudson's Bay Company's rights to Vancouver Island revoked
Battle of Booneville MS - captured Gen Beauregard evacuates Corinth
Battle of Bethesda Church [Totopotomoy Creek], cavalry battle fought in Hanover County, Virginia, inconclusive result (US Civil War)
Opera "Die Verkaufte Braut" premieres (Prague)
"Decoration Day," later called Memorial Day, is first observed in Northern US states
Ottoman sultan Abd-ul-Aziz is deposed and succeeded by his nephew Murat V.
92°F highest temperature ever recorded in Cleveland in May
An F4 tornado strikes Irving, Kansas, killing 18 and injuring 60.
Gilmore Garden (NYC) renamed Madison Square Garden
First baseman Dave Foutz hits Brooklyn Bridegrooms' (later Dodgers) first ever home run during doubleheader defeats to Chicago Colts at Washington Park, Brooklyn
Boston Beaneaters baseball second baseman Bobby Lowe first to hit 4 home runs in MLB game in 20-11 win against Cincinnati Reds
First car accident occurs; Henry Wells hits a cyclist in NYC
Hall of Fame for Great Americans dedicated on NYU campus
Spanish King Alfonso XII, who was elected as a constitutional monarch, suspends the Cortes, Spain's parliament
Frank Chancegets hit by pitch 5 times in a doubleheader
The Japanese Army capture the City of Dairen after landing troops along the south coast of Manchuria
Aldrich Vineland Currency Act forerunner to Federal Reserve System
First federal workers' compensation law is approved
Paris advocate E Archdeacon is 1st passenger in a airplane
US Assay Office in Salt Lake City, Utah authorized
Reuben Siegel lays the cornerstone of the first home in Tel Aviv
1st Indianapolis 500: Ray Harroun, driving a Marmon Wasp for Nordyke & Marmon Company, comes out of retirement and wins the inaugural event with an average speed of 74.6 mph (120.1 km/h)
US Marines sent to Nicaragua
John McGraw joins Fred Clarke, Cap Anson, Frank Selee, & Connie Mack as managers who have won 1,000 games
Treaty of London is signed by the Great Powers, the Ottoman Empire, and the victorious Balkan League (Serbia, Greece, the Kingdom of Bulgaria, and Montenegro), bringing an end to the First Balkan War
The new and then largest Cunard ocean liner RMS Aquitania, 45,647 tons, sets sails on her maiden voyage from Liverpool, England to New York City.
Jazz standard "Darktown Strutters' Ball" by Shelton Brooks is recorded by the Original Dixieland Jazz Band
Lord Dunsany's "If" premieres in London
Memorial to Capt. Eddie Grant, killed in WW I, unveiled at Polo Grounds, NYC
Salzburg, Austria, votes to join Germany

Cubs and Cardinals trade outfielders between morning and afternoon games of doubleheader; Max Flack goes to St. Louis, Cliff Heathcote heads to Chicago Cubs; both get hits for new clubs in the nightcap
Latvia & Vatican sign accord
Socialist Matteotti falls in Italian parliament by fascists
British mariners shoot on demonstrators
In China protests erupt against the Great Powers infringing on Chinese sovereignty.
Peter DePaolo became 1st man to average over 100 mph at Indy
Shedd Aquarium, one of the first inland aquariums in the world, opens in Chicago, Illinois
PhilliesChuck Kleinhomers off Ben Cantwell (Braves) in both ends DH
Yankees dedicate a plaque to Miller Huggins
Patent on invisible glass installation
Memorial Day Massacre: Chicago Police Department shoot and kill 10 unarmed demonstrators during the "Little Steel Strike" in the United States
New York Yankees sweep arch rival Boston Red Sox, 10-0 & 5-4 in front of 83,533 at Yankee Stadium
1st anti semitic measures in Serbia
British Army enters Baghdad, chasing pro-German coup government
1,047 bombers bomb Cologne in RAF's raid of WW II
US aircraft carrier Yorktown leaves Pearl Harbor
US troops reconquer Attu Aleutians
Bama Rowell hits a home run in a baseball match - the ball shatters Bulova Clock in Ebbets Field in Brooklyn, New York
United flight 521 crashes on takeoff at LaGuardia Airport (NY) 42 die
A dike along the flooding Columbia River breaks, obliterating Vanport, Oregon within minutes. Fifteen people die and tens of thousands are left homeless.
East German constitution approved
NPS/VHP win 1st general election in Suriname
WRTV TV channel 6 in Indianapolis, IN (ABC) begins broadcasting
Charlie Grimm succeeds Tommy Holmes as manager of Boston Braves
1st major league network baseball game-Cleveland 7, Chicago 2
Dutch bishops forbid membership to non-catholic sporting clubs
Emile Zatopek runs world record 5K (13:57.2)
Hector Villa-Lobos' "Odisseia de Uma Raca" premieres
KMVT TV channel 11 in Twin Falls, ID (CBS/NBC/ABC) begins broadcasting
Said el-Mufti forms Jordan government
Tunisia begins domestic self governing
Bus boycott begins in Tallahassee Florida
US performs nuclear test at Enewetak Atoll (atmospheric tests)
Test Cricket debut for Rohan Kanhai v England at Edgbaston
Unidentified soldiers killed in WW II & Korean War buried in Arlington
US performs nuclear test at Enewetak Atoll (atmospheric tests)
Iraq withdraws from the Baghdad Pact (Iran, Iraq, Pakistan, Turkey, and the United Kingdom)
President Luis Somoza Debayle ends emergency crisis in Nicaragua
President Stroessner disbands Paraguay's parliament
The Auckland Harbour Bridge is officially opened in Auckland, New Zealand.
Dutch DC-8 crashes after takeoff at Lisbon, 62 die
Maris hits his 10th & 11th of 61 HRs
69 killed in bus crash in Ahmedabad, India
MLB San Francisco Giants sweep double-header against New York Mets 5-3 & 8-6 in combined 23 innings; records include elapsed time of 9:50, 47 strikeouts, 7:22 for 2nd game & NY's 22 K's in two games
The Beatles' "Love Me Do" single goes #1 in the United States
France performs nuclear test at Ecker Algeria (Underground)
Viet Cong offensive against US base Da Nang, begins
Vivian Malone is 1st black to graduate from University of Alabama
300 US airplanes bomb North Vietnam
The Beatles release single "Paperback Writer" / "Rain" in US; "Rain" featured experimental studio tricks of slowed down bass and drums tracks, and backwards vocals in the fade out
US launches Surveyor 1 to Moon
King Hussein of Jordan visits Cairo
Republic of Biafra, a predominantly Igbo secessionist state in eastern Nigeria, is founded by Lt. Col. Odumegwu Ojukwu
Beatles begin work on their only double album "The Beatles" - popularly known as "The White Album"
President De Gaulle disbands French parliament
The Paulinerkirche, a church built in 1231 at the University of Leipzig, is demolished by the East German communist regime to make way for new development; protesters are arrested
West German Parliament accepts emergency crisis law
Australian Derek Clayton runs world record marathon (2:08:33.6) at Antwerp, Belgium; record disputed (short course)
Gibraltar adopts constitution
Riots by oil workers striking against economic and racial disparities in Willemstad, Curacao, Netherlands Antilles spark calls for the island's independence
Baseball All-Star voting is returned to fans
36 hospitalized during Grateful Dead concert; drunk LSD apple juice
Train crash at Duivendrecht, Netherlands, 5 die
US Mariner 9 1st satellite to orbit Mars launched
3 Japanese PFL terrorists kills 24, wound 72 at Tel Aviv's Lod Intl airport
The Angry Brigade goes on trial over a series of 25 bombings throughout Britain.
George Harrisonreleases his fourth studio album "Living in the Material World" in the US
European Space Agency (ESA) forms
Cleveland Indian Dennis Eckersley no-hits California Angels, 2-0
31st Cannes Film Festival: "The Tree of Wooden Clogs" directed by Ermanno Olmi wins the Palme d'Or
Percom Data Company Inc release Microdos for Radio Shack's TRS-80
Ted Coombs begins a 5,193 mile roller skate from LA to NYC
1st papal visit to France since 1814
Tiger reliever John Hiller, 37, (who had a 1971 heart attack), retires
Turner's painting "Juliet & Her Nurse" sells for $6.4 million
Twins Ken Landreaux ends his hitting streak after 31 games
"Nightline" extends from 4 nights to 5 nights a week (Friday)
LA Dodgers are quickest to get 1,000,000 attendance in a season in only 22 games
Spain becomes 16th member of NATO
Surrey all out for 14 vs Essex, their lowest score ever
Bomb explodes in rebel leader Edén Pastora's headquarters in Nicaragua
Ariane-2 (ESA) launched
France performs nuclear test
The North American Philips Company unveils the compact disc video format (CD-V), which can hold up to 800 MB, enough for a full-length movie
Tony Tucker TKOs Buster Douglas in 10 for heavyweight boxing title
Dow Jones Industrial Average hits a record 2,878.56
64th National Spelling Bee: Joanne Lagatta wins spelling 'antipyretic'
Mexican-American Arturo Barrios runs world record one-hour distance, covering 21.101 km; record broken in 2007
US Supreme Court rules prosecutors can be sued for legal advice they give police and can be held accountable  (Burns v, Reed)
NY Lotto pays $30 million to one winner (#s are 12-15-30-33-40-48)
NY Yankee Scott Sanderson becomes 9th to beat all 26 teams
UN votes for sanctions against Serb-led Yugoslavia to halt fighting
69th National Spelling Bee: Wendy Guey wins spelling vivisepulture
Albert Belleuses a forearm to break up a double play & nearly breaks Brewer 2nd baseman Fernando Vina's nose, Belle gets 2 game suspension
John Tesh's final day as host of "Entertainment Tonight"
A magnitude 6.6 earthquake hits northern Afghanistan, killing up to 5,000.
Super Rugby Final, Eden Park, Auckland: In an all-NZ final, Andrew Mehrtens lands 2 penalties & 2 conversions as the Canterbury Crusaders beat the Blues, 20-13; Crusaders' first title

Super Rugby Final, Carisbrook, Dunedin: Canterbury Crusaders retain title with a 24-19 win over Otago Highlanders; flyhalf Andrew Mehrtens kicks 3 penalties, a conversion & dropped goal for the winners
Super Rugby Final, Pretoria: Morné Steyn kicks 5 conversions & 2 penalties as the Bulls thump the Chiefs (Waikato, NZ), 61-17
A number of nations including Germany, Turkey and Canada, expel Syrian diplomats following the Houla massacre
Vishwanathan Anand wins his fifth World Chess Championship
German EmperorHenry IVcrowns his son Conrad II, King of Germany
Hundred Years' War: 19-year-oldJoan of Arcis burned at the stake by an English-dominated tribunal in Rouen, France
Coronation asMargaret of Anjouas Queen Consort of England at Westminster Abbey
Christopher Columbusdeparts with 6 ships for 3rd trip to America
Portuguese forces underAfonso de Albuquerqueabandon Goa after its former ruler Yusuf Adil Shah, the Muslim King of Bijapur reconquers the city
King Henry IIIfollows brotherCharles IXas King of France

The Peace of Prague signed between the Habsburg EmperorFerdinand IIand the Electorate of Saxony (representing Protestant states of the Holy Roman Empire). It effectively ended the civil war aspect of the Thirty Years' War
All honours granted byCharles Iare retrospectively annulled by Parliament
Margaret Cavendish is the first woman to attend a meeting of the Royal Society, watching demonstrations by Robert Boyle andRobert Hooke
Future US PresidentAndrew Jacksonkills Charles Dickinson in a duel after Dickinson accused Jackson's wife of bigamy
Napoleon Bonaparteannexes Tuscany and gives it seats in the French Senate
John Francis attempts to assassinateQueen Victoria
Hershey Park, a family leisure playground founded byMilton S. Hersheyfor the exclusive use of his employees, opens in Hershey, Pennsylvania
CompletedLincoln Memorialdedicated by US Chief JusticeWilliam Howard Taftin front of 50,000
Howard Hanson's 1st Symphony "Nordic" premieres
Walter Johnsonrecords 110th and final shutout of his Baseball HOF career, the most in MLB history; Washington Senators score 3-0 win over Boston Red Sox
Philadelphia pitcher Jim Bivin retiresBabe Ruthon an infield grounder in "the Babe's" final MLB at-bat; Ruth plays just 1 inning in Boston Braves, 11-6 loss to Phillies at the Baker Bowl
Walter Piston's dance work, The Incredible Flutist, written for the Boston Pops Orchestra, which premieres it, Arthur Fiedler conducting
Reichsfuehrer HermanHimmlerarrives in Prague
Satchel Paigepitches 5 innings to defeat Dizzy Dean All-Stars 8-1
French GeneralCharles De Gaullearrives in Algiers

Transport No. 75 departs Drancy (France) internment camp, taking 1,000 French Jews toAuschwitzextermination camp in Nazi German occupied Poland; 627 killed upon arrival, approximately 99 survive the war
Ezzard Charlesbeats Joey Maxim in 15 for heavyweight boxing title
Mickey Mantlemisses by 18 inches hitting 1st home run out of Yankee Stadium in the first game of a doubleheader against the Washington Senators
Long time Dominican dictatorRafael Trujillois assassinated in Santo Domingo, Dominican Republic.
Benjamin Britten's "War Requiem" incorporating the poems ofWilfred Owenpremieres, marking the consecration of the new Coventry Cathedral
The Beatles 1961 recording of an original instrumental "Cry for a Shadow" is #1 in Australia; it is the only song credited to songwriting team ofGeorge HarrisonandJohn Lennon
American motorcycle daredevil Robert "Evel"Knieveljumps 16 automobiles on his motorcycle in Gardena, California
Argentinian authorGabriel García Márquez's most important work "One Hundred Years of Solitude" is published in Buenos Aires
YankeeWhitey Ford, nearing 41, announces his retirement from baseball
TigersAl Kalinecollides with another player & swallows his tongue
Willie Mayshits his 638th HR, sets NL record of 1,950 runs scored
Capitol Records releases "Venus and Mars", the fourth studio album byPaul McCartney& Wings in the US
AL President Lee MacPhail suspends Yankees ownerGeorge Steinbrennerfor one week, for his public criticism of umpires

Stanley Cup Final, Northlands Coliseum, Edmonton, AL: Edmonton Oilers take back-to-back titles; beat Philadelphia Flyers, 8-3 for a 4-1 series victory;Wayne GretzkyandJari Kurrieach post a goal and 3 assists
Barry Bondsmakes his MLB debut for the Pittsburgh Pirates
Mike Tysonbeats Pinklon Thomas by TKO in round 6 in Las Vegas to retain WBC / WBA heavyweight boxing titles
Margaret Ray pleads guilty to breaking intoDavid Letterman's house
Betty Shabazz, widow of Malcolm X, set on fire by 12-year-old grandson
Ken Drydenbecomes president of NHL's Toronto Maple Leafs
Animated fish tale "Finding Nemo", directed by Andrew Stanton, and starring Albert Brooks andEllen DeGenerespremieres in US and Canada
Gordon Ramsayfirst appears on US television in cooking competition show "Hell's Kitchen" on Fox
Spanish explorerHernando de Soto's expedition of 10 ships and 700 men lands in Florida
Stampede caused by a rumor that the Brooklyn Bridge was going to collapse kills 12
